theory.canonical_statement: The the1pi DOCX is a categorical verification protocol. Its source code constructs a verification topos, a cohomological signature, an Atiyah-Singer spectral invariant, a Grothendieck class, a modular form, and a final verification tensor. The packet expresses that tensor in a printable mathematical carrier while retaining the source extraction path.

dictionary_sections.definition: the1pi is repaired here as a source-backed registry entry centered on the relation \mathcal{V}_{\pi}=\left(C_{\Omega}\otimes I_{\mathrm{AS}}\otimes K_{0}(\Omega_{\mathrm{verification}})\otimes f_{\Delta}\right)\otimes R_{\mu\nu\rho\sigma}. The repair replaces a missing, duplicated, or malformed registry field with the exact mathematical object found in the cited source family.
dictionary_sections.contextual_meaning: The the1pi DOCX is a categorical verification protocol. Its source code constructs a verification topos, a cohomological signature, an Atiyah-Singer spectral invariant, a Grothendieck class, a modular form, and a final verification tensor. The packet expresses that tensor in a printable mathematical carrier while retaining the source extraction path. The dictionary page should remain word-rich: it explains why the formula belongs to this ID, while the equation support page carries the lean algebraic chain.
dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: The immediate support chain is c(E)=\prod_{i=1}^{\operatorname{rank}}(1+x_{i}); \operatorname{Td}(\Omega_{\mathrm{verification}})=\prod_{i}\frac{x_{i}}{1-e^{-x_{i}}}; \operatorname{Confidence}=1-\exp\left(-I_{\mathrm{AS}}^{2}/2\right). These relations give the derivation page a local sequence rather than a disconnected endpoint.
dictionary_sections.lemma_support: The lemma support is source lineage: the1pi.docx supplies the mathematical relation, and the duplicate/malformed ID is repaired by preserving that relation with explicit provenance.

\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=1.6em,itemsep=6pt,topsep=4pt]
\item Source repair: confirmed equation substituted for an empty or malformed registry field.
\item Canonical carrier: the selected formula that anchors the entry.
\item Equation support: the local progression used to derive or contextualize the carrier.
\end{enumerate}
